hypothesis: If catalytic surface compositions are generated using the CompGen framework — a Chemically Informed Autoencoder (CIAE) latent space plus a conditional latent diffusion model pretrained on OC20 and fine-tuned on HEA — then the generated 2D shell-wise compositions will more closely match the real composition distributions (lower Fréchet Distance), better satisfy allowed-element constraints (lower Leakage), and achieve higher point-wise similarity (higher Cosine Similarity) than compositions produced by a chemically-agnostic baseline or unpretrained models, and will retain superior performance under reduced training-data regimes, because the PTR-informed CIAE latent space encodes chemical priors that enable more accurate conditioned sampling.
